ayahs.textArabic: كٓهيعٓصٓ
ayahs.textTransliteration: KafhayaAAaynsad
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Kaf, Ha, Ya, 'Ayn, Sad.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Kãf-Ha-Ya-’Aĩn- Ṣãd.

ayahs.textArabic: ذِكۡرُ رَحۡمَتِ رَبِّكَ عَبۡدَهُۥ زَكَرِيَّآ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Thikru rahmati rabbika AAabdahu zakariyya
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [This is] a mention of the mercy of your Lord to His servant Zechariah
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹This is˺ a reminder of your Lord’s mercy to His servant Zachariah,

ayahs.textArabic: إِذۡ نَادَىٰ رَبَّهُۥ نِدَآءً خَفِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ith nada rabbahu nidaan khafiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: When he called to his Lord a private supplication.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: when he cried out to his Lord privately,

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ رَبِّ إِنِّي وَهَنَ ٱلۡعَظۡمُ مِنِّي وَٱشۡتَعَلَ ٱلرَّأۡسُ شَيۡبٗا وَلَمۡ أَكُنۢ بِدُعَآئِكَ رَبِّ شَقِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala rabbi innee wahana alAAathmu minnee waishtaAAala alrrasu shayban walam akun biduAAaika rabbi shaqiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He said, "My Lord, indeed my bones have weakened, and my head has filled with white, and never have I been in my supplication to You, my Lord, unhappy.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: saying, “My Lord! Surely my bones have become brittle, and grey hair has spread across my head, but I have never been disappointed in my prayer to You, my Lord!

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِنِّي خِفۡتُ ٱلۡمَوَٰلِيَ مِن وَرَآءِي وَكَانَتِ ٱمۡرَأَتِي عَاقِرٗا فَهَبۡ لِي مِن لَّدُنكَ وَلِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wainnee khiftu almawaliya min waraee wakanati imraatee AAaqiran fahab lee min ladunka waliyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, I fear the successors after me, and my wife has been barren, so give me from Yourself an heir
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And I am concerned about ˹the faith of˺ my relatives after me, since my wife is barren. So grant me, by Your grace, an heir,

ayahs.textArabic: يَرِثُنِي وَيَرِثُ مِنۡ ءَالِ يَعۡقُوبَۖ وَٱجۡعَلۡهُ رَبِّ رَضِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Yarithunee wayarithu min ali yaAAqooba waijAAalhu rabbi radiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Who will inherit me and inherit from the family of Jacob. And make him, my Lord, pleasing [to You]."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: who will inherit ˹prophethood˺ from me and the family of Jacob, and make him, O Lord, pleasing ˹to You˺!”

ayahs.textArabic: يَٰزَكَرِيَّآ إِنَّا نُبَشِّرُكَ بِغُلَٰمٍ ٱسۡمُهُۥ يَحۡيَىٰ لَمۡ نَجۡعَل لَّهُۥ مِن قَبۡلُ سَمِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ya zakariyya inna nubashshiruka bighulamin ismuhu yahya lam najAAal lahu min qablu samiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [He was told], "O Zechariah, indeed We give you good tidings of a boy whose name will be John. We have not assigned to any before [this] name."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹The angels announced,˺ “O Zachariah! Indeed, We give you the good news of ˹the birth of˺ a son, whose name will be John—a name We have not given to anyone before.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ رَبِّ أَنَّىٰ يَكُونُ لِي غُلَٰمٞ وَكَانَتِ ٱمۡرَأَتِي عَاقِرٗا وَقَدۡ بَلَغۡتُ مِنَ ٱلۡكِبَرِ عِتِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala rabbi anna yakoonu lee ghulamun wakanati imraatee AAaqiran waqad balaghtu mina alkibari AAitiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He said, "My Lord, how will I have a boy when my wife has been barren and I have reached extreme old age?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He wondered, “My Lord! How can I have a son when my wife is barren, and I have become extremely old?”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ كَذَٰلِكَ قَالَ رَبُّكَ هُوَ عَلَيَّ هَيِّنٞ وَقَدۡ خَلَقۡتُكَ مِن قَبۡلُ وَلَمۡ تَكُ شَيۡـٔٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala kathalika qala rabbuka huwa AAalayya hayyinun waqad khalaqtuka min qablu walam taku shayan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [An angel] said, "Thus [it will be]; your Lord says, 'It is easy for Me, for I created you before, while you were nothing.' "
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: An angel replied, “So will it be! Your Lord says, ‘It is easy for Me, just as I created you before, when you were nothing!’”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ رَبِّ ٱجۡعَل لِّيٓ ءَايَةٗۖ قَالَ ءَايَتُكَ أَلَّا تُكَلِّمَ ٱلنَّاسَ ثَلَٰثَ لَيَالٖ سَوِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala rabbi ijAAal lee ayatan qala ayatuka alla tukallima alnnasa thalatha layalin sawiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Zechariah] said, "My Lord, make for me a sign." He said, "Your sign is that you will not speak to the people for three nights, [being] sound."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Zachariah said, “My Lord! Grant me a sign.” He responded, “Your sign is that you will not ˹be able to˺ speak to people for three nights, despite being healthy.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَخَرَجَ عَلَىٰ قَوۡمِهِۦ مِنَ ٱلۡمِحۡرَابِ فَأَوۡحَىٰٓ إِلَيۡهِمۡ أَن سَبِّحُواْ بُكۡرَةٗ وَعَشِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fakharaja AAala qawmihi mina almihrabi faawha ilayhim an sabbihoo bukratan waAAashiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So he came out to his people from the prayer chamber and signaled to them to exalt [Allah] in the morning and afternoon.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So he came out to his people from the sanctuary, signalling to them to glorify ˹Allah˺ morning and evening.

ayahs.textArabic: يَٰيَحۡيَىٰ خُذِ ٱلۡكِتَٰبَ بِقُوَّةٖۖ وَءَاتَيۡنَٰهُ ٱلۡحُكۡمَ صَبِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ya yahya khuthi alkitaba biquwwatin waataynahu alhukma sabiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Allah] said, "O John, take the Scripture with determination." And We gave him judgement [while yet] a boy
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹It was later said,˺ “O John! Hold firmly to the Scriptures.” And We granted him wisdom while ˹he was still˺ a child,

ayahs.textArabic: وَحَنَانٗا مِّن لَّدُنَّا وَزَكَوٰةٗۖ وَكَانَ تَقِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wahananan min ladunna wazakatan wakana taqiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And affection from Us and purity, and he was fearing of Allah
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: as well as purity and compassion from Us. And he was God-fearing,

ayahs.textArabic: وَبَرَّۢا بِوَٰلِدَيۡهِ وَلَمۡ يَكُن جَبَّارًا عَصِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wabarran biwalidayhi walam yakun jabbaran AAasiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And dutiful to his parents, and he was not a disobedient tyrant.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and kind to his parents. He was neither arrogant nor disobedient.

ayahs.textArabic: وَسَلَٰمٌ عَلَيۡهِ يَوۡمَ وُلِدَ وَيَوۡمَ يَمُوتُ وَيَوۡمَ يُبۡعَثُ حَيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wasalamun AAalayhi yawma wulida wayawma yamootu wayawma yubAAathu hayyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And peace be upon him the day he was born and the day he dies and the day he is raised alive.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Peace be upon him the day he was born, and the day of his death, and the day he will be raised back to life!

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱذۡكُرۡ فِي ٱلۡكِتَٰبِ مَرۡيَمَ إِذِ ٱنتَبَذَتۡ مِنۡ أَهۡلِهَا مَكَانٗا شَرۡقِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waothkur fee alkitabi maryama ithi intabathat min ahliha makanan sharqiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And mention, [O Muhammad], in the Book [the story of] Mary, when she withdrew from her family to a place toward the east.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And mention in the Book ˹O Prophet, the story of˺ Mary when she withdrew from her family to a place in the east,

ayahs.textArabic: فَٱتَّخَذَتۡ مِن دُونِهِمۡ حِجَابٗا فَأَرۡسَلۡنَآ إِلَيۡهَا رُوحَنَا فَتَمَثَّلَ لَهَا بَشَرٗا سَوِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faittakhathat min doonihim hijaban faarsalna ilayha roohana fatamaththala laha basharan sawiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And she took, in seclusion from them, a screen. Then We sent to her Our Angel, and he represented himself to her as a well-proportioned man.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: screening herself off from them. Then We sent to her Our angel, ˹Gabriel,˺ appearing before her as a man, perfectly formed.

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَتۡ إِنِّيٓ أَعُوذُ بِٱلرَّحۡمَٰنِ مِنكَ إِن كُنتَ تَقِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qalat innee aAAoothu bialrrahmani minka in kunta taqiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: She said, "Indeed, I seek refuge in the Most Merciful from you, [so leave me], if you should be fearing of Allah."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: She appealed, “I truly seek refuge in the Most Compassionate from you! ˹So leave me alone˺ if you are God-fearing.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ إِنَّمَآ أَنَا۠ رَسُولُ رَبِّكِ لِأَهَبَ لَكِ غُلَٰمٗا زَكِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala innama ana rasoolu rabbiki liahaba laki ghulaman zakiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He said, "I am only the messenger of your Lord to give you [news of] a pure boy."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He responded, “I am only a messenger from your Lord, ˹sent˺ to bless you with a pure son.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَتۡ أَنَّىٰ يَكُونُ لِي غُلَٰمٞ وَلَمۡ يَمۡسَسۡنِي بَشَرٞ وَلَمۡ أَكُ بَغِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qalat anna yakoonu lee ghulamun walam yamsasnee basharun walam aku baghiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: She said, "How can I have a boy while no man has touched me and I have not been unchaste?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: She wondered, “How can I have a son when no man has ever touched me, nor am I unchaste?”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ كَذَٰلِكِ قَالَ رَبُّكِ هُوَ عَلَيَّ هَيِّنٞۖ وَلِنَجۡعَلَهُۥٓ ءَايَةٗ لِّلنَّاسِ وَرَحۡمَةٗ مِّنَّاۚ وَكَانَ أَمۡرٗا مَّقۡضِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala kathaliki qala rabbuki huwa AAalayya hayyinun walinajAAalahu ayatan lilnnasi warahmatan minna wakana amran maqdiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He said, "Thus [it will be]; your Lord says, 'It is easy for Me, and We will make him a sign to the people and a mercy from Us. And it is a matter [already] decreed.' "
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He replied, “So will it be! Your Lord says, ‘It is easy for Me. And so will We make him a sign for humanity and a mercy from Us.’ It is a matter ˹already˺ decreed.”

ayahs.textArabic: ۞ فَحَمَلَتۡهُ فَٱنتَبَذَتۡ بِهِۦ مَكَانٗا قَصِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fahamalathu faintabathat bihi makanan qasiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So she conceived him, and she withdrew with him to a remote place.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So she conceived him and withdrew with him to a remote place.

ayahs.textArabic: فَأَجَآءَهَا ٱلۡمَخَاضُ إِلَىٰ جِذۡعِ ٱلنَّخۡلَةِ قَالَتۡ يَٰلَيۡتَنِي مِتُّ قَبۡلَ هَٰذَا وَكُنتُ نَسۡيٗا مَّنسِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faajaaha almakhadu ila jithAAi alnnakhlati qalat ya laytanee mittu qabla hatha wakuntu nasyan mansiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And the pains of childbirth drove her to the trunk of a palm tree. She said, "Oh, I wish I had died before this and was in oblivion, forgotten."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then the pains of labour drove her to the trunk of a palm tree. She cried, “Alas! I wish I had died before this, and was a thing long forgotten!”

ayahs.textArabic: فَنَادَىٰهَا مِن تَحۡتِهَآ أَلَّا تَحۡزَنِي قَدۡ جَعَلَ رَبُّكِ تَحۡتَكِ سَرِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fanadaha min tahtiha alla tahzanee qad jaAAala rabbuki tahtaki sariyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But he called her from below her, "Do not grieve; your Lord has provided beneath you a stream.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So a voice reassured her from below her, “Do not grieve! Your Lord has provided a stream at your feet.

ayahs.textArabic: وَهُزِّيٓ إِلَيۡكِ بِجِذۡعِ ٱلنَّخۡلَةِ تُسَٰقِطۡ عَلَيۡكِ رُطَبٗا جَنِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wahuzzee ilayki bijithAAi alnnakhlati tusaqit AAalayki rutaban janiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And shake toward you the trunk of the palm tree; it will drop upon you ripe, fresh dates.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And shake the trunk of this palm tree towards you, it will drop fresh, ripe dates upon you.

ayahs.textArabic: فَكُلِي وَٱشۡرَبِي وَقَرِّي عَيۡنٗاۖ فَإِمَّا تَرَيِنَّ مِنَ ٱلۡبَشَرِ أَحَدٗا فَقُولِيٓ إِنِّي نَذَرۡتُ لِلرَّحۡمَٰنِ صَوۡمٗا فَلَنۡ أُكَلِّمَ ٱلۡيَوۡمَ إِنسِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fakulee waishrabee waqarree AAaynan faimma tarayinna mina albashari ahadan faqoolee innee nathartu lilrrahmani sawman falan okallima alyawma insiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So eat and drink and be contented. And if you see from among humanity anyone, say, 'Indeed, I have vowed to the Most Merciful abstention, so I will not speak today to [any] man.' "
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So eat and drink, and put your heart at ease. But if you see any of the people, say, ‘I have vowed silence to the Most Compassionate, so I am not talking to anyone today.’”

ayahs.textArabic: فَأَتَتۡ بِهِۦ قَوۡمَهَا تَحۡمِلُهُۥۖ قَالُواْ يَٰمَرۡيَمُ لَقَدۡ جِئۡتِ شَيۡـٔٗا فَرِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faatat bihi qawmaha tahmiluhu qaloo ya maryamu laqad jiti shayan fariyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then she brought him to her people, carrying him. They said, "O Mary, you have certainly done a thing unprecedented.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then she returned to her people, carrying him. They said ˹in shock˺, “O Mary! You have certainly done a horrible thing!

ayahs.textArabic: يَٰٓأُخۡتَ هَٰرُونَ مَا كَانَ أَبُوكِ ٱمۡرَأَ سَوۡءٖ وَمَا كَانَتۡ أُمُّكِ بَغِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ya okhta haroona ma kana abooki imraa sawin wama kanat ommuki baghiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: O sister of Aaron, your father was not a man of evil, nor was your mother unchaste."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: O sister of Aaron! Your father was not an indecent man, nor was your mother unchaste.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَأَشَارَتۡ إِلَيۡهِۖ قَالُواْ كَيۡفَ نُكَلِّمُ مَن كَانَ فِي ٱلۡمَهۡدِ صَبِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faasharat ilayhi qaloo kayfa nukallimu man kana fee almahdi sabiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So she pointed to him. They said, "How can we speak to one who is in the cradle a child?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So she pointed to the baby. They exclaimed, “How can we talk to someone who is an infant in the cradle?”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ إِنِّي عَبۡدُ ٱللَّهِ ءَاتَىٰنِيَ ٱلۡكِتَٰبَ وَجَعَلَنِي نَبِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala innee AAabdu Allahi ataniya alkitaba wajaAAalanee nabiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Jesus] said, "Indeed, I am the servant of Allah. He has given me the Scripture and made me a prophet.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Jesus declared, “I am truly a servant of Allah. He has destined me to be given the Scripture and to be a prophet.

ayahs.textArabic: وَجَعَلَنِي مُبَارَكًا أَيۡنَ مَا كُنتُ وَأَوۡصَٰنِي بِٱلصَّلَوٰةِ وَٱلزَّكَوٰةِ مَا دُمۡتُ حَيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: WajaAAalanee mubarakan ayna ma kuntu waawsanee bialssalati waalzzakati ma dumtu hayyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And He has made me blessed wherever I am and has enjoined upon me prayer and zakah as long as I remain alive
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He has made me a blessing wherever I go, and bid me to establish prayer and give alms-tax as long as I live,

ayahs.textArabic: وَبَرَّۢا بِوَٰلِدَتِي وَلَمۡ يَجۡعَلۡنِي جَبَّارٗا شَقِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wabarran biwalidatee walam yajAAalnee jabbaran shaqiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [made me] dutiful to my mother, and He has not made me a wretched tyrant.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and to be kind to my mother. He has not made me arrogant or defiant.

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱلسَّلَٰمُ عَلَيَّ يَوۡمَ وُلِدتُّ وَيَوۡمَ أَمُوتُ وَيَوۡمَ أُبۡعَثُ حَيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waalssalamu AAalayya yawma wulidtu wayawma amootu wayawma obAAathu hayyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And peace is on me the day I was born and the day I will die and the day I am raised alive."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Peace be upon me the day I was born, the day I die, and the day I will be raised back to life!”

ayahs.textArabic: ذَٰلِكَ عِيسَى ٱبۡنُ مَرۡيَمَۖ قَوۡلَ ٱلۡحَقِّ ٱلَّذِي فِيهِ يَمۡتَرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Thalika AAeesa ibnu maryama qawla alhaqqi allathee feehi yamtaroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: That is Jesus, the son of Mary - the word of truth about which they are in dispute.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: That is Jesus, son of Mary. ˹And this is˺ a word of truth, about which they dispute.

ayahs.textArabic: مَا كَانَ لِلَّهِ أَن يَتَّخِذَ مِن وَلَدٖۖ سُبۡحَٰنَهُۥٓۚ إِذَا قَضَىٰٓ أَمۡرٗا فَإِنَّمَا يَقُولُ لَهُۥ كُن فَيَكُونُ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ma kana lillahi an yattakhitha min waladin subhanahu itha qada amran fainnama yaqoolu lahu kun fayakoonu
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: It is not [befitting] for Allah to take a son; exalted is He! When He decrees an affair, He only says to it, "Be," and it is.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: It is not for Allah to take a son! Glory be to Him. When He decrees a matter, He simply tells it, “Be!” And it is!

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ رَبِّي وَرَبُّكُمۡ فَٱعۡبُدُوهُۚ هَٰذَا صِرَٰطٞ مُّسۡتَقِيمٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wainna Allaha rabbee warabbukum faoAAbudoohu hatha siratun mustaqeemun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Jesus said], "And indeed, Allah is my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him. That is a straight path."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Jesus also declared,˺ “Surely Allah is my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him ˹alone˺. This is the Straight Path.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَٱخۡتَلَفَ ٱلۡأَحۡزَابُ مِنۢ بَيۡنِهِمۡۖ فَوَيۡلٞ لِّلَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ مِن مَّشۡهَدِ يَوۡمٍ عَظِيمٍ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faikhtalafa alahzabu min baynihim fawaylun lillatheena kafaroo min mashhadi yawmin AAatheemin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then the factions differed [concerning Jesus] from among them, so woe to those who disbelieved - from the scene of a tremendous Day.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Yet their ˹various˺ groups have differed among themselves ˹about him˺, so woe to the disbelievers when they face a tremendous Day!

ayahs.textArabic: أَسۡمِعۡ بِهِمۡ وَأَبۡصِرۡ يَوۡمَ يَأۡتُونَنَا لَٰكِنِ ٱلظَّٰلِمُونَ ٱلۡيَوۡمَ فِي ضَلَٰلٖ مُّبِينٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: AsmiAA bihim waabsir yawma yatoonana lakini alththalimoona alyawma fee dalalin mubeenin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: How [clearly] they will hear and see the Day they come to Us, but the wrongdoers today are in clear error.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: How clearly will they hear and see on the Day they will come to Us! But today the wrongdoers are clearly astray.

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَنذِرۡهُمۡ يَوۡمَ ٱلۡحَسۡرَةِ إِذۡ قُضِيَ ٱلۡأَمۡرُ وَهُمۡ فِي غَفۡلَةٖ وَهُمۡ لَا يُؤۡمِنُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waanthirhum yawma alhasrati ith qudiya alamru wahum fee ghaflatin wahum la yuminoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And warn them, [O Muhammad], of the Day of Regret, when the matter will be concluded; and [yet], they are in [a state of] heedlessness, and they do not believe.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And warn them ˹O Prophet˺ of the Day of Regret, when all matters will be settled, while they are ˹engrossed˺ in heedlessness and disbelief.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّا نَحۡنُ نَرِثُ ٱلۡأَرۡضَ وَمَنۡ عَلَيۡهَا وَإِلَيۡنَا يُرۡجَعُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna nahnu narithu alarda waman AAalayha wailayna yurjaAAoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, it is We who will inherit the earth and whoever is on it, and to Us they will be returned.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, it is We Who will succeed the earth and whoever is on it. And to Us they will ˹all˺ be returned.

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱذۡكُرۡ فِي ٱلۡكِتَٰبِ إِبۡرَٰهِيمَۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ صِدِّيقٗا نَّبِيًّا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waothkur fee alkitabi ibraheema innahu kana siddeeqan nabiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And mention in the Book [the story of] Abraham. Indeed, he was a man of truth and a prophet.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And mention in the Book ˹O Prophet, the story of˺ Abraham. He was surely a man of truth and a prophet.

ayahs.textArabic: إِذۡ قَالَ لِأَبِيهِ يَٰٓأَبَتِ لِمَ تَعۡبُدُ مَا لَا يَسۡمَعُ وَلَا يُبۡصِرُ وَلَا يُغۡنِي عَنكَ شَيۡـٔٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ith qala liabeehi ya abati lima taAAbudu ma la yasmaAAu wala yubsiru wala yughnee AAanka shayan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Mention] when he said to his father, "O my father, why do you worship that which does not hear and does not see and will not benefit you at all?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Remember˺ when he said to his father, “O dear father! Why do you worship what can neither hear nor see, nor benefit you at all?

ayahs.textArabic: يَٰٓأَبَتِ إِنِّي قَدۡ جَآءَنِي مِنَ ٱلۡعِلۡمِ مَا لَمۡ يَأۡتِكَ فَٱتَّبِعۡنِيٓ أَهۡدِكَ صِرَٰطٗا سَوِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ya abati innee qad jaanee mina alAAilmi ma lam yatika faittabiAAnee ahdika siratan sawiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: O my father, indeed there has come to me of knowledge that which has not come to you, so follow me; I will guide you to an even path.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: O dear father! I have certainly received some knowledge which you have not received, so follow me and I will guide you to the Straight Path.

ayahs.textArabic: يَٰٓأَبَتِ لَا تَعۡبُدِ ٱلشَّيۡطَٰنَۖ إِنَّ ٱلشَّيۡطَٰنَ كَانَ لِلرَّحۡمَٰنِ عَصِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ya abati la taAAbudi alshshaytana inna alshshaytana kana lilrrahmani AAasiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: O my father, do not worship Satan. Indeed Satan has ever been, to the Most Merciful, disobedient.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: O dear father! Do not worship Satan. Surely Satan is ever rebellious against the Most Compassionate.

ayahs.textArabic: يَٰٓأَبَتِ إِنِّيٓ أَخَافُ أَن يَمَسَّكَ عَذَابٞ مِّنَ ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنِ فَتَكُونَ لِلشَّيۡطَٰنِ وَلِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ya abati innee akhafu an yamassaka AAathabun mina alrrahmani fatakoona lilshshyatani waliyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: O my father, indeed I fear that there will touch you a punishment from the Most Merciful so you would be to Satan a companion [in Hellfire]."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: O dear father! I truly fear that you will be touched by a torment from the Most Compassionate, and become Satan’s companion ˹in Hell˺.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ أَرَاغِبٌ أَنتَ عَنۡ ءَالِهَتِي يَٰٓإِبۡرَٰهِيمُۖ لَئِن لَّمۡ تَنتَهِ لَأَرۡجُمَنَّكَۖ وَٱهۡجُرۡنِي مَلِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala araghibun anta AAan alihatee ya ibraheemu lain lam tantahi laarjumannaka waohjurnee maliyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [His father] said, "Have you no desire for my gods, O Abraham? If you do not desist, I will surely stone you, so avoid me a prolonged time."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He threatened, “How dare you reject my idols, O Abraham! If you do not desist, I will certainly stone you ˹to death˺. So be gone from me for a long time!”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ سَلَٰمٌ عَلَيۡكَۖ سَأَسۡتَغۡفِرُ لَكَ رَبِّيٓۖ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ بِي حَفِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala salamun AAalayka saastaghfiru laka rabbee innahu kana bee hafiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Abraham] said, "Peace will be upon you. I will ask forgiveness for you of my Lord. Indeed, He is ever gracious to me.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Abraham responded, “Peace be upon you! I will pray to my Lord for your forgiveness. He has truly been Most Gracious to me.

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَعۡتَزِلُكُمۡ وَمَا تَدۡعُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ وَأَدۡعُواْ رَبِّي عَسَىٰٓ أَلَّآ أَكُونَ بِدُعَآءِ رَبِّي شَقِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: WaaAAtazilukum wama tadAAoona min dooni Allahi waadAAoo rabbee AAasa alla akoona biduAAai rabbee shaqiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And I will leave you and those you invoke other than Allah and will invoke my Lord. I expect that I will not be in invocation to my Lord unhappy."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: As I distance myself from ˹all of˺ you and from whatever you invoke besides Allah, I will ˹continue to˺ call upon my Lord ˹alone˺, trusting that I will never be disappointed in invoking my Lord.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَلَمَّا ٱعۡتَزَلَهُمۡ وَمَا يَعۡبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ وَهَبۡنَا لَهُۥٓ إِسۡحَٰقَ وَيَعۡقُوبَۖ وَكُلّٗا جَعَلۡنَا نَبِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Falamma iAAtazalahum wama yaAAbudoona min dooni Allahi wahabna lahu ishaqa wayaAAqooba wakullan jaAAalna nabiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So when he had left them and those they worshipped other than Allah, We gave him Isaac and Jacob, and each [of them] We made a prophet.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So after he had left them and what they worshipped besides Allah, We granted him Isaac and Jacob, and made each of them a prophet.

ayahs.textArabic: وَوَهَبۡنَا لَهُم مِّن رَّحۡمَتِنَا وَجَعَلۡنَا لَهُمۡ لِسَانَ صِدۡقٍ عَلِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wawahabna lahum min rahmatina wajaAAalna lahum lisana sidqin AAaliyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We gave them of Our mercy, and we made for them a reputation of high honor.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We showered them with Our mercy, and blessed them with honourable mention.

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱذۡكُرۡ فِي ٱلۡكِتَٰبِ مُوسَىٰٓۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ مُخۡلَصٗا وَكَانَ رَسُولٗا نَّبِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waothkur fee alkitabi moosa innahu kana mukhlasan wakana rasoolan nabiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And mention in the Book, Moses. Indeed, he was chosen, and he was a messenger and a prophet.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And mention in the Book ˹O Prophet, the story of˺ Moses. He was truly a chosen man, and was a messenger and a prophet.

ayahs.textArabic: وَنَٰدَيۡنَٰهُ مِن جَانِبِ ٱلطُّورِ ٱلۡأَيۡمَنِ وَقَرَّبۡنَٰهُ نَجِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wanadaynahu min janibi alttoori alaymani waqarrabnahu najiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We called him from the side of the mount at [his] right and brought him near, confiding [to him].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We called him from the right side of Mount Ṭûr, and drew him near, speaking ˹with him˺ directly.

ayahs.textArabic: وَوَهَبۡنَا لَهُۥ مِن رَّحۡمَتِنَآ أَخَاهُ هَٰرُونَ نَبِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wawahabna lahu min rahmatina akhahu haroona nabiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We gave him out of Our mercy his brother Aaron as a prophet.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And We appointed for him—out of Our grace—his brother, Aaron, as a prophet.

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱذۡكُرۡ فِي ٱلۡكِتَٰبِ إِسۡمَٰعِيلَۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ صَادِقَ ٱلۡوَعۡدِ وَكَانَ رَسُولٗا نَّبِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waothkur fee alkitabi ismaAAeela innahu kana sadiqa alwaAAdi wakana rasoolan nabiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And mention in the Book, Ishmael. Indeed, he was true to his promise, and he was a messenger and a prophet.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And mention in the Book ˹O Prophet, the story of˺ Ishmael. He was truly a man of his word, and was a messenger and a prophet.

ayahs.textArabic: وَكَانَ يَأۡمُرُ أَهۡلَهُۥ بِٱلصَّلَوٰةِ وَٱلزَّكَوٰةِ وَكَانَ عِندَ رَبِّهِۦ مَرۡضِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wakana yamuru ahlahu bialssalati waalzzakati wakana AAinda rabbihi mardiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And he used to enjoin on his people prayer and zakah and was to his Lord pleasing.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He used to urge his people to pray and give alms-tax. And his Lord was well pleased with him.

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱذۡكُرۡ فِي ٱلۡكِتَٰبِ إِدۡرِيسَۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ صِدِّيقٗا نَّبِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waothkur fee alkitabi idreesa innahu kana siddeeqan nabiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And mention in the Book, Idrees. Indeed, he was a man of truth and a prophet.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And mention in the Book ˹O Prophet, the story of˺ Enoch. He was surely a man of truth and a prophet.

ayahs.textArabic: وَرَفَعۡنَٰهُ مَكَانًا عَلِيًّا
ayahs.textTransliteration: WarafaAAnahu makanan AAaliyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We raised him to a high station.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And We elevated him to an honourable status.

ayahs.textArabic: أُوْلَٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ أَنۡعَمَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيۡهِم مِّنَ ٱلنَّبِيِّـۧنَ مِن ذُرِّيَّةِ ءَادَمَ وَمِمَّنۡ حَمَلۡنَا مَعَ نُوحٖ وَمِن ذُرِّيَّةِ إِبۡرَٰهِيمَ وَإِسۡرَٰٓءِيلَ وَمِمَّنۡ هَدَيۡنَا وَٱجۡتَبَيۡنَآۚ إِذَا تُتۡلَىٰ عَلَيۡهِمۡ ءَايَٰتُ ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنِ خَرُّواْۤ سُجَّدٗاۤ وَبُكِيّٗا۩
ayahs.textTransliteration: Olaika allatheena anAAama Allahu AAalayhim mina alnnabiyyeena min thurriyyati adama wamimman hamalna maAAa noohin wamin thurriyyati ibraheema waisraeela wamimman hadayna waijtabayna itha tutla AAalayhim ayatu alrrahmani kharroo sujjadan wabukiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Those were the ones upon whom Allah bestowed favor from among the prophets of the descendants of Adam and of those We carried [in the ship] with Noah, and of the descendants of Abraham and Israel, and of those whom We guided and chose. When the verses of the Most Merciful were recited to them, they fell in prostration and weeping.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Those were ˹some of˺ the prophets who Allah has blessed from among the descendants of Adam, and of those We carried with Noah ˹in the Ark˺, and of the descendants of Abraham and Israel, and of those We ˹rightly˺ guided and chose. Whenever the revelations of the Most Compassionate were recited to them, they fell down, prostrating and weeping.

ayahs.textArabic: ۞ فَخَلَفَ مِنۢ بَعۡدِهِمۡ خَلۡفٌ أَضَاعُواْ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَٱتَّبَعُواْ ٱلشَّهَوَٰتِۖ فَسَوۡفَ يَلۡقَوۡنَ غَيًّا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fakhalafa min baAAdihim khalfun adaAAoo alssalata waittabaAAoo alshshahawati fasawfa yalqawna ghayyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But there came after them successors who neglected prayer and pursued desires; so they are going to meet evil -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But they were succeeded by generations who neglected prayer and followed their lusts and so will soon face the evil consequences.

ayahs.textArabic: إِلَّا مَن تَابَ وَءَامَنَ وَعَمِلَ صَٰلِحٗا فَأُوْلَٰٓئِكَ يَدۡخُلُونَ ٱلۡجَنَّةَ وَلَا يُظۡلَمُونَ شَيۡـٔٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Illa man taba waamana waAAamila salihan faolaika yadkhuloona aljannata wala yuthlamoona shayan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Except those who repent, believe and do righteousness; for those will enter Paradise and will not be wronged at all.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: As for those who repent, believe, and do good, it is they who will be admitted into Paradise, never being denied any reward.

ayahs.textArabic: جَنَّٰتِ عَدۡنٍ ٱلَّتِي وَعَدَ ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنُ عِبَادَهُۥ بِٱلۡغَيۡبِۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ وَعۡدُهُۥ مَأۡتِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Jannati AAadnin allatee waAAada alrrahmanu AAibadahu bialghaybi innahu kana waAAduhu matiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Therein are] gardens of perpetual residence which the Most Merciful has promised His servants in the unseen. Indeed, His promise has ever been coming.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹They will be in˺ the Gardens of Eternity, promised in trust by the Most Compassionate to His servants. Surely His promise will be fulfilled.

ayahs.textArabic: لَّا يَسۡمَعُونَ فِيهَا لَغۡوًا إِلَّا سَلَٰمٗاۖ وَلَهُمۡ رِزۡقُهُمۡ فِيهَا بُكۡرَةٗ وَعَشِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: La yasmaAAoona feeha laghwan illa salaman walahum rizquhum feeha bukratan waAAashiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They will not hear therein any ill speech - only [greetings of] peace - and they will have their provision therein, morning and afternoon.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: There they will never hear any idle talk—only ˹greetings of˺ peace. And there they will have their provisions morning and evening.

ayahs.textArabic: تِلۡكَ ٱلۡجَنَّةُ ٱلَّتِي نُورِثُ مِنۡ عِبَادِنَا مَن كَانَ تَقِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Tilka aljannatu allatee noorithu min AAibadina man kana taqiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: That is Paradise, which We give as inheritance to those of Our servants who were fearing of Allah.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: That is Paradise, which We will grant to whoever is devout among Our servants.

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَا نَتَنَزَّلُ إِلَّا بِأَمۡرِ رَبِّكَۖ لَهُۥ مَا بَيۡنَ أَيۡدِينَا وَمَا خَلۡفَنَا وَمَا بَيۡنَ ذَٰلِكَۚ وَمَا كَانَ رَبُّكَ نَسِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wama natanazzalu illa biamri rabbika lahu ma bayna aydeena wama khalfana wama bayna thalika wama kana rabbuka nasiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Gabriel said], "And we [angels] descend not except by the order of your Lord. To Him belongs that before us and that behind us and what is in between. And never is your Lord forgetful -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: “We only descend by the command of your Lord. To Him belongs whatever is before us, and whatever is behind us, and everything in between. And your Lord is never forgetful.

ayahs.textArabic: رَّبُّ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضِ وَمَا بَيۡنَهُمَا فَٱعۡبُدۡهُ وَٱصۡطَبِرۡ لِعِبَٰدَتِهِۦۚ هَلۡ تَعۡلَمُ لَهُۥ سَمِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Rabbu alssamawati waalardi wama baynahuma faoAAbudhu waistabir liAAibadatihi hal taAAlamu lahu samiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them - so worship Him and have patience for His worship. Do you know of any similarity to Him?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹He is the˺ Lord of the heavens, and the earth, and everything in between. So worship Him ˹alone˺, and be steadfast in His worship. Do you know of anyone equal to Him ˹in His attributes˺?”

ayahs.textArabic: وَيَقُولُ ٱلۡإِنسَٰنُ أَءِذَا مَا مِتُّ لَسَوۡفَ أُخۡرَجُ حَيًّا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wayaqoolu alinsanu aitha ma mittu lasawfa okhraju hayyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And the disbeliever says, "When I have died, am I going to be brought forth alive?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Yet ˹some˺ people ask ˹mockingly˺, “After I die, will I really be raised to life again?”

ayahs.textArabic: أَوَلَا يَذۡكُرُ ٱلۡإِنسَٰنُ أَنَّا خَلَقۡنَٰهُ مِن قَبۡلُ وَلَمۡ يَكُ شَيۡـٔٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Awala yathkuru alinsanu anna khalaqnahu min qablu walam yaku shayan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Does man not remember that We created him before, while he was nothing?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Do ˹such˺ people not remember that We created them before, when they were nothing?

ayahs.textArabic: فَوَرَبِّكَ لَنَحۡشُرَنَّهُمۡ وَٱلشَّيَٰطِينَ ثُمَّ لَنُحۡضِرَنَّهُمۡ حَوۡلَ جَهَنَّمَ جِثِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fawarabbika lanahshurannahum waalshshayateena thumma lanuhdirannahum hawla jahannama jithiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So by your Lord, We will surely gather them and the devils; then We will bring them to be present around Hell upon their knees.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: By your Lord ˹O Prophet˺! We will surely gather them along with the devils, and then set them around Hell on their knees.

ayahs.textArabic: ثُمَّ لَنَنزِعَنَّ مِن كُلِّ شِيعَةٍ أَيُّهُمۡ أَشَدُّ عَلَى ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنِ عِتِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Thumma lananziAAanna min kulli sheeAAatin ayyuhum ashaddu AAala alrrahmani AAitiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then We will surely extract from every sect those of them who were worst against the Most Merciful in insolence.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then We will certainly begin by dragging out of every group the ones most defiant to the Most Compassionate.

ayahs.textArabic: ثُمَّ لَنَحۡنُ أَعۡلَمُ بِٱلَّذِينَ هُمۡ أَوۡلَىٰ بِهَا صِلِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Thumma lanahnu aAAlamu biallatheena hum awla biha siliyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then, surely it is We who are most knowing of those most worthy of burning therein.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And We truly know best who is most deserving of burning in it.

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِن مِّنكُمۡ إِلَّا وَارِدُهَاۚ كَانَ عَلَىٰ رَبِّكَ حَتۡمٗا مَّقۡضِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wain minkum illa wariduha kana AAala rabbika hatman maqdiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And there is none of you except he will come to it. This is upon your Lord an inevitability decreed.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: There is none of you who will not pass over it. ˹This is˺ a decree your Lord must fulfil.

ayahs.textArabic: ثُمَّ نُنَجِّي ٱلَّذِينَ ٱتَّقَواْ وَّنَذَرُ ٱلظَّٰلِمِينَ فِيهَا جِثِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Thumma nunajjee allatheena ittaqaw wanatharu alththalimeena feeha jithiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then We will save those who feared Allah and leave the wrongdoers within it, on their knees.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then We will deliver those who were devout, leaving the wrongdoers there on their knees.

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِذَا تُتۡلَىٰ عَلَيۡهِمۡ ءَايَٰتُنَا بَيِّنَٰتٖ قَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ لِلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓاْ أَيُّ ٱلۡفَرِيقَيۡنِ خَيۡرٞ مَّقَامٗا وَأَحۡسَنُ نَدِيّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waitha tutla AAalayhim ayatuna bayyinatin qala allatheena kafaroo lillatheena amanoo ayyu alfareeqayni khayrun maqaman waahsanu nadiyyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And when Our verses are recited to them as clear evidences, those who disbelieve say to those who believe, "Which of [our] two parties is best in position and best in association?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: When Our clear revelations are recited to them, the disbelievers ask the believers ˹mockingly˺, “Which of the two of us is better in position and superior in assembly?”

ayahs.textArabic: وَكَمۡ أَهۡلَكۡنَا قَبۡلَهُم مِّن قَرۡنٍ هُمۡ أَحۡسَنُ أَثَٰثٗا وَرِءۡيٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wakam ahlakna qablahum min qarnin hum ahsanu athathan wariyan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And how many a generation have We destroyed before them who were better in possessions and [outward] appearance?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Imagine, O Prophet˺ how many peoples We have destroyed before them, who were far better in luxury and splendour!

ayahs.textArabic: قُلۡ مَن كَانَ فِي ٱلضَّلَٰلَةِ فَلۡيَمۡدُدۡ لَهُ ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنُ مَدًّاۚ حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا رَأَوۡاْ مَا يُوعَدُونَ إِمَّا ٱلۡعَذَابَ وَإِمَّا ٱلسَّاعَةَ فَسَيَعۡلَمُونَ مَنۡ هُوَ شَرّٞ مَّكَانٗا وَأَضۡعَفُ جُندٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qul man kana fee alddalalati falyamdud lahu alrrahmanu maddan hatta itha raaw ma yooAAadoona imma alAAathaba waimma alssaAAata fasayaAAlamoona man huwa sharrun makanan waadAAafu jundan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Say, "Whoever is in error - let the Most Merciful extend for him an extension [in wealth and time] until, when they see that which they were promised - either punishment [in this world] or the Hour [of resurrection] - they will come to know who is worst in position and weaker in soldiers."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “Whoever is ˹entrenched˺ in misguidance, the Most Compassionate will allow them plenty of time, until—behold!—they face what they are threatened with: either the torment or the Hour. Only then will they realize who is worse in position and inferior in manpower.”

ayahs.textArabic: وَيَزِيدُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱهۡتَدَوۡاْ هُدٗىۗ وَٱلۡبَٰقِيَٰتُ ٱلصَّٰلِحَٰتُ خَيۡرٌ عِندَ رَبِّكَ ثَوَابٗا وَخَيۡرٞ مَّرَدًّا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wayazeedu Allahu allatheena ihtadaw hudan waalbaqiyatu alssalihatu khayrun AAinda rabbika thawaban wakhayrun maraddan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And Allah increases those who were guided, in guidance, and the enduring good deeds are better to your Lord for reward and better for recourse.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And Allah increases in guidance those who are ˹rightly˺ guided. And the everlasting good deeds are far better with your Lord in reward and in outcome.

ayahs.textArabic: أَفَرَءَيۡتَ ٱلَّذِي كَفَرَ بِـَٔايَٰتِنَا وَقَالَ لَأُوتَيَنَّ مَالٗا وَوَلَدًا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Afaraayta allathee kafara biayatina waqala laootayanna malan wawaladan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then, have you seen he who disbelieved in Our verses and said, "I will surely be given wealth and children [in the next life]?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Have you seen ˹O Prophet˺ the one who rejects Our revelations yet boasts, “I will definitely be granted ˹plenty of˺ wealth and children ˹if there is an afterlife˺.”?

ayahs.textArabic: أَطَّلَعَ ٱلۡغَيۡبَ أَمِ ٱتَّخَذَ عِندَ ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنِ عَهۡدٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: AttalaAAa alghayba ami ittakhatha AAinda alrrahmani AAahdan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Has he looked into the unseen, or has he taken from the Most Merciful a promise?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Has he looked into the unseen or taken a pledge from the Most Compassionate?

ayahs.textArabic: كـَلَّاۚ سَنَكۡتُبُ مَا يَقُولُ وَنَمُدُّ لَهُۥ مِنَ ٱلۡعَذَابِ مَدّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Kalla sanaktubu ma yaqoolu wanamuddu lahu mina alAAathabi maddan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: No! We will record what he says and extend for him from the punishment extensively.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Not at all! We certainly record whatever he claims and will increase his punishment extensively.

ayahs.textArabic: وَنَرِثُهُۥ مَا يَقُولُ وَيَأۡتِينَا فَرۡدٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wanarithuhu ma yaqoolu wayateena fardan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We will inherit him [in] what he mentions, and he will come to Us alone.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And We will inherit what he boasts of, and he will come before Us all by himself.

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱتَّخَذُواْ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ ءَالِهَةٗ لِّيَكُونُواْ لَهُمۡ عِزّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waittakhathoo min dooni Allahi alihatan liyakoonoo lahum AAizzan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they have taken besides Allah [false] deities that they would be for them [a source of] honor.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They have taken other gods, instead of Allah, seeking strength ˹and protection˺ through them.

ayahs.textArabic: كـَلَّاۚ سَيَكۡفُرُونَ بِعِبَادَتِهِمۡ وَيَكُونُونَ عَلَيۡهِمۡ ضِدًّا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Kalla sayakfuroona biAAibadatihim wayakoonoona AAalayhim diddan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: No! Those "gods" will deny their worship of them and will be against them opponents [on the Day of Judgement].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But no! Those ˹gods˺ will deny their worship and turn against them.

ayahs.textArabic: أَلَمۡ تَرَ أَنَّآ أَرۡسَلۡنَا ٱلشَّيَٰطِينَ عَلَى ٱلۡكَٰفِرِينَ تَؤُزُّهُمۡ أَزّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Alam tara anna arsalna alshshayateena AAala alkafireena taozzuhum azzan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Do you not see that We have sent the devils upon the disbelievers, inciting them to [evil] with [constant] incitement?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Do you ˹O Prophet˺ not see that We have sent the devils against the disbelievers, constantly inciting them?

ayahs.textArabic: فَلَا تَعۡجَلۡ عَلَيۡهِمۡۖ إِنَّمَا نَعُدُّ لَهُمۡ عَدّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fala taAAjal AAalayhim innama naAAuddu lahum AAaddan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So be not impatient over them. We only count out to them a [limited] number.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So do not be in haste against them, for indeed We are ˹closely˺ counting down their days.

ayahs.textArabic: يَوۡمَ نَحۡشُرُ ٱلۡمُتَّقِينَ إِلَى ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنِ وَفۡدٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Yawma nahshuru almuttaqeena ila alrrahmani wafdan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: On the Day We will gather the righteous to the Most Merciful as a delegation
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Watch for˺ the Day We will gather the righteous before the Most Compassionate as an honoured delegation,

ayahs.textArabic: وَنَسُوقُ ٱلۡمُجۡرِمِينَ إِلَىٰ جَهَنَّمَ وِرۡدٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wanasooqu almujrimeena ila jahannama wirdan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And will drive the criminals to Hell in thirst
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and drive the wicked to Hell like a thirsty herd.

ayahs.textArabic: لَّا يَمۡلِكُونَ ٱلشَّفَٰعَةَ إِلَّا مَنِ ٱتَّخَذَ عِندَ ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنِ عَهۡدٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: La yamlikoona alshshafaAAata illa mani ittakhatha AAinda alrrahmani AAahdan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: None will have [power of] intercession except he who had taken from the Most Merciful a covenant.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: None will have the right to intercede, except those who have taken a covenant from the Most Compassionate.

ayahs.textArabic: وَقَالُواْ ٱتَّخَذَ ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنُ وَلَدٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waqaloo ittakhatha alrrahmanu waladan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they say, "The Most Merciful has taken [for Himself] a son."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They say, “The Most Compassionate has offspring.”

ayahs.textArabic: لَّقَدۡ جِئۡتُمۡ شَيۡـًٔا إِدّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Laqad jitum shayan iddan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: You have done an atrocious thing.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: You have certainly made an outrageous claim,

ayahs.textArabic: تَكَادُ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتُ يَتَفَطَّرۡنَ مِنۡهُ وَتَنشَقُّ ٱلۡأَرۡضُ وَتَخِرُّ ٱلۡجِبَالُ هَدًّا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Takadu alssamawatu yatafattarna minhu watanshaqqu alardu watakhirru aljibalu haddan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The heavens almost rupture therefrom and the earth splits open and the mountains collapse in devastation
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: by which the heavens are about to burst, the earth to split apart, and the mountains to crumble to pieces

ayahs.textArabic: أَن دَعَوۡاْ لِلرَّحۡمَٰنِ وَلَدٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: An daAAaw lilrrahmani waladan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: That they attribute to the Most Merciful a son.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: in protest of attributing children to the Most Compassionate.

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَا يَنۢبَغِي لِلرَّحۡمَٰنِ أَن يَتَّخِذَ وَلَدًا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wama yanbaghee lilrrahmani an yattakhitha waladan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And it is not appropriate for the Most Merciful that He should take a son.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: It does not befit ˹the majesty of˺ the Most Compassionate to have children.

ayahs.textArabic: إِن كُلُّ مَن فِي ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضِ إِلَّآ ءَاتِي ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنِ عَبۡدٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: In kullu man fee alssamawati waalardi illa atee alrrahmani AAabdan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: There is no one in the heavens and earth but that he comes to the Most Merciful as a servant.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: There is none in the heavens or the earth who will not return to the Most Compassionate in full submission.

ayahs.textArabic: لَّقَدۡ أَحۡصَىٰهُمۡ وَعَدَّهُمۡ عَدّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Laqad ahsahum waAAaddahum AAaddan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He has enumerated them and counted them a [full] counting.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, He fully knows them and has counted them precisely.

ayahs.textArabic: وَكُلُّهُمۡ ءَاتِيهِ يَوۡمَ ٱلۡقِيَٰمَةِ فَرۡدًا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wakulluhum ateehi yawma alqiyamati fardan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And all of them are coming to Him on the Day of Resurrection alone.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And each of them will return to Him on the Day of Judgment all alone.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ وَعَمِلُواْ ٱلصَّٰلِحَٰتِ سَيَجۡعَلُ لَهُمُ ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنُ وُدّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna allatheena amanoo waAAamiloo alssalihati sayajAAalu lahumu alrrahmanu wuddan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, those who have believed and done righteous deeds - the Most Merciful will appoint for them affection.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: As for those who believe and do good, the Most Compassionate will ˹certainly˺ bless them with ˹genuine˺ love.

ayahs.textArabic: فَإِنَّمَا يَسَّرۡنَٰهُ بِلِسَانِكَ لِتُبَشِّرَ بِهِ ٱلۡمُتَّقِينَ وَتُنذِرَ بِهِۦ قَوۡمٗا لُّدّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fainnama yassarnahu bilisanika litubashshira bihi almuttaqeena watunthira bihi qawman luddan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So, [O Muhammad], We have only made Qur'an easy in the Arabic language that you may give good tidings thereby to the righteous and warn thereby a hostile people.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, We have made this ˹Quran˺ easy in your own language ˹O Prophet˺ so with it you may give good news to the righteous and warn those who are contentious.

ayahs.textArabic: وَكَمۡ أَهۡلَكۡنَا قَبۡلَهُم مِّن قَرۡنٍ هَلۡ تُحِسُّ مِنۡهُم مِّنۡ أَحَدٍ أَوۡ تَسۡمَعُ لَهُمۡ رِكۡزَۢا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wakam ahlakna qablahum min qarnin hal tuhissu minhum min ahadin aw tasmaAAu lahum rikzan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And how many have We destroyed before them of generations? Do you perceive of them anyone or hear from them a sound?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Imagine˺ how many peoples We have destroyed before them! Do you ˹still˺ see any of them, or ˹even˺ hear from them the slightest sound?
